ledger.destination: Hot Springs Medical and SPA, Banya, Bulgaria
ledger.source: Paying $168-$185 per night for a 4-star regional spa hotel is a classic absolute-value trap, as the local market offers comparable thermal resort utility for a family at significantly lower price floors.
ledger.note: High absolute baseline price masquerading as a seasonal deal; local dining and extras will inflate costs further.

ledger.destination: Arte Spa & Park Hotel, Velingrad, Bulgaria
ledger.source: At roughly €145/night, this falls directly into the absolute-value trap for Velingrad, where the framed discount is irrelevant because the absolute price buys nothing exceptional compared to cheaper regional competitors.
ledger.note: Peak-season price anchoring disguising a completely standard, unexceptional absolute rate for the region.

ledger.destination: Alexandra Beach Thassos Spa Resort, Potos, Thassos, Greece
ledger.source: The 3.5-hour drive plus a 40-minute ferry crossing pushes door-to-door transit well past the 4-hour threshold, adding too much logistical friction and toddler tax for a completely standard shoulder-season rate.
ledger.note: Ferry wait times, boarding logistics, and island driving will push total travel time past 5 hours, defeating the purpose of a low-friction getaway.

ledger.destination: Kempinski Hotel Grand Arena, Bansko, Bulgaria
ledger.note: Removed poisoned baseline: Stage 3 verified July 26-27 pricing (€158) for a September window at low confidence — out-of-window, unreliable.

ledger.source: At €105/night, this triggers the absolute-value trap; it is a completely standard off-peak rate for premium Velingrad spa hotels rather than a rare arbitrage opportunity.
ledger.note: September is actually a high-demand transition period for Bulgarian spa tourism, meaning local dining and extras will not reflect low-season pricing.

ledger.source: The €95/night rate is unremarkable for this massive resort in autumn, and unpredictable mountain weather risks eliminating the utility of outdoor activities for a toddler.
ledger.note: Mountain lifts are frequently reduced to weekend-only operations by mid-September, severely limiting weekday utility.
Verified Lucky Bansko Aparthotel SPA & Relax, 5-star for Sep 10-17, 2026: €96.21/night (€673.46 total, 7 nights), review 9.1. Price matches the Stage-1 estimate.

ledger.destination: Velingrad, Park Hotel and SPA Vella Hills
ledger.source: Fails the absolute-value floor test; €105/night is a completely standard, unremarkable rate for a 4-star spa hotel in Velingrad rather than a true arbitrage opportunity.
ledger.note: Standard regional pricing masquerading as a seasonal discount.

ledger.source: While highly convenient, €98/night is simply the normal market rate for Hisarya spa hotels, lacking the massive price drop required for a high-utility value play.
ledger.note: Lack of genuine discount; ordinary absolute pricing for the geography.

ledger.source: Driving to Sofia, airport waits, the flight, and local transfers will push total door-to-door transit past 5 hours, while low-cost airline fees for luggage and family seating will erase the hotel savings.
ledger.note: Budget flight 'toddler tax' (mandatory seating/baggage fees) and transit fatigue.
